summary.current_primary_after_stage103: Stage71 retry2 Stage57+Stage66 bridge-enabled frozen recovery
summary.not_promotable_reason: Stage102 policy was selected using VitaminC external labels; it is diagnostic evidence, not a valid tuned deployment rule.
summary.main_interpretation: Stage102/103 shows that Stage92C contains a recoverable latent SUPPORT signal. The best diagnostic policy changes NE predictions to SUPPORT, improving accuracy, macro-F1, SUPPORT recall, and false-NE count while preserving REFUTE recall. This indicates the failure mode is not only representation; it is partly a final entitlement/routing threshold problem.
recommendations: Do not promote the Stage102 threshold policy.
Record threshold feasibility as diagnostic evidence.
Stop synthetic bridge append search unless a new mechanism is designed.
Next work should be clean-only threshold/mechanism design or paper/report synthesis.

risks.mitigation: Treat Stage102/103 as diagnostic only. Do not use the selected target=440 as a deployed threshold.
Any future mechanism must be derived from clean-dev/internal split only and re-run clean preservation before external.
Prefer mechanism design based on margins/calibration learned without external labels rather than hard target support count from VitaminC.
Best diagnostic policy lowers false_NE but raises false_entitlement vs Stage73 by +4; guardrails required.

next_options.description: Design a clean-dev/internal-only threshold mechanism. No external labels. Must not use Stage102 selected value as a tuned threshold.
Patch runner to always export external prediction rows for future diagnostics. Static code patch only; no Kaggle run inside prompt.
Write report section: bridge training alters REFUTE/SUPPORT tradeoff, while logits reveal recoverable under-entitled SUPPORT signal.
